title: 📚 Sacred Tongue Tokenizer System - Complete Reference
content:
# 📚 Sacred Tongue Tokenizer System - Complete Reference

Sacred Tongue Tokenizer System - Complete Reference
Purpose: Central hub for the Six Sacred Tongues lexicons, encoding algorithms, and implementation guides
Version: 2.0 (Corrected)
Last Updated: January 26, 2026
Overview
The Sacred Tongue Tokenizer System provides deterministic, bijective encoding of arbitrary byte sequences into pronounceable "spell-text" using six constructed languages (conlangs). Each tongue serves a specific cryptographic domain in the SCBE-AETHERMOORE security stack.
Core Innovation
Nibble-based encoding: Every byte (0x00-0xFF) maps to exactly one token per tongue:
token = prefixes[byte >> 4] + "'" + suffixes[byte & 0x0F]
High nibble (bits 4-7): Selects 1 of 16 prefixes
Low nibble (bits 0-3): Selects 1 of 16 suffixes
Result: 16 × 16 = 256 unique tokens per tongue
Security Properties
✅ Deterministic: Same byte always produces same token
✅ Bijective: Perfect 1-to-1 mapping (lossless roundtrip)
✅ Collision-free: No two bytes map to the same token
✅ Constant-time: O(1) encode/decode with lookup tables
✅ Domain-separated: Different message fields use different tongues
The Six Sacred Tongues
Tokenizer Implementation
Python Reference Implementation
class SacredTongueTokenizer:
    """Encode/decode bytes to Sacred Tongue spell-text."""
    
    def __init__(self, tongue_code: str):
        self.tongue_code = tongue_code
        self.prefixes = TONGUES[tongue_code]['prefixes']
        self.suffixes = TONGUES[tongue_code]['suffixes']
        self._build_lookup_tables()
    
    def _build_lookup_tables(self):
        # Forward: byte → token
        self._byte_to_token = []
        for b in range(256):
            prefix_idx = b >> 4
            suffix_idx = b & 0x0F
            token = f"{self.prefixes[prefix_idx]}'{self.suffixes[suffix_idx]}"
            self._byte_to_token.append(token)
        
        # Reverse: token → byte
        self._token_to_byte = {
            token: b for b, token in enumerate(self._byte_to_token)
        }
    
    def encode_byte(self, b: int) -> str:
        """Encode single byte to token."""
        return self._byte_to_token[b]
    
    def decode_token(self, token: str) -> int:
        """Decode token back to byte."""
        return self._token_to_byte[token]
    
    def encode(self, data: bytes) -> str:
        """Encode byte string to spell-text."""
        tokens = [self.encode_byte(b) for b in data]
        return ' '.join(f"{self.tongue_code}:{t}" for t in tokens)
    
    def decode(self, spell_text: str) -> bytes:
        """Decode spell-text back to bytes."""
        tokens = [t.split(':')[1] for t in spell_text.split()]
        return bytes([self.decode_token(t) for t in tokens])
Verification Property
# For all tongues and all bytes:
for tongue in ['ko', 'av', 'ru', 'ca', 'um', 'dr']:
    tokenizer = SacredTongueTokenizer(tongue)
    for b in range(256):
        original = bytes([b])
        encoded = tokenizer.encode(original)
        decoded = tokenizer.decode(encoded)
        assert decoded == original  # Must always pass
Tutorial & Examples
Step-by-step manual encoding, programmatic usage, and verification
Integration with SCBE-AETHERMOORE
SS1 Blob Format
Sacred Tongues power the SpiralSeal SS1 encoding layer:
SS1|kid=<key_id>|aad=<context>|<salt_spell>|<nonce_spell>|<ct_spell>|<tag_spell>
Example:
SS1|kid=k01|aad=service=prod|ru:khar'ak drath'eth|ko:sil'an ra'il|ca:bip'a bop'e klik'i|dr:anvil'a tharn'e
Domain Separation
Different message components use different tongues for semantic clarity:
Salt (RU): Binding material for key derivation
Nonce (KO): Flow control for encryption
Ciphertext (CA): Transformed data
Tag (DR): Structural integrity proof
Version History
v2.0 (January 26, 2026) - CURRENT
Critical Fix: Corrected Kor'aelin (KO) wordlist to eliminate:
Duplicate tokens (e.g., "ra'en" for both 0x1e and 0x15)
Prefix conflicts (e.g., "ael" for high nibbles 7 and 8)
Suffix conflicts (e.g., "un" for low nibbles 8 and 12)
New KO prefixes: vor (8), fir (10), dun (14), pyr (15)
New KO suffixes: an (0), is (5), ur (6), ak (8), ol (9), ek (13)
Impact: All 256 KO tokens now unique and deterministic
v1.0 (January 18, 2026)
Initial implementation with 6 tongues (contained KO conflicts)
